scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU (KARNATAKA)

O.S. No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}}/2024

BETWEEN:

SMT. ANITA SHARMA,
Age: 52 years,
Residing at Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

... PLAINTIFF

AND:

MR. RAJESH REDDY,
Age: 60 years,
Residing at HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

... DEFENDANT

SUITS FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT

The Plaintiff above named submits as follows:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has jurisdiction to try and entertain this suit as the subject matter of the suit, being immovable property situated at Koramangala, Bengaluru, falls within the territorial limits of this Court. The Defendant also resides within Bengaluru. The value of the suit for the purpose of jurisdiction is Rs. 85,00,000/-, which is within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court. This suit is filed under Section 9 and Section 16 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

FACTS OF THE CASE

1. The Plaintiff is a resident of Indiranagar, Bengaluru, and is seeking enforcement of a valid contract for sale entered into with the Defendant.

2. The Defendant is the absolute owner of the property described in the Schedule of Property hereinbelow, being Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru.

3. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell regarding the Schedule Property for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-.

4. On 10.01.2024, pursuant to the Agreement to Sell, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant via RTGS bearing {{UTR_NUMBER}} HDFC20240110987654.

5. As per the terms of the Agreement to Sell, the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against execution and registration of the Sale Deed.

READINESS AND WILLINGNESS

1. The Plaintiff states that she has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract under Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

2. The Plaintiff was ready and willing to pay the balance consideration on or before 10.07.2024 as agreed.

3. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff refusing to execute the sale deed, claiming that he had received a higher offer for the Schedule Property.

4. On 20.06.2024, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed in terms of the Agreement to Sell.

5. The Defendant refused to comply with the legal notice and has failed to execute the sale deed despite the Plaintiff's readiness and willingness to perform.

LEGAL BASIS

1. The Plaintiff is entitled to specific performance of the contract under Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, as the contract is in writing and the Defendant has refused to perform.

2. The Plaintiff has performed and has always been ready and willing to perform the essential terms of the contract as required under Section 16 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

3. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation in lieu of or in addition to specific performance under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

4. The Plaintiff is entitled to additional reliefs including possession under Section 22 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

5. The contract is valid and enforceable under {{CONTRACT_ACT_PROVISION}}.

6. The Plaintiff's rights are further protected under Section 53A of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882.

CAUSE OF ACTION

1. The cause of action arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant refused to execute the sale deed. The cause of action is a single event and does not continue day-to-day.

LIMITATION

1. The suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years for specific performance of a contract from the date fixed for performance or when the plaintiff receives notice that performance is refused.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

1. The suit is valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee.

2. The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid on the plaint under the Karnataka Court-Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

PRAYER

1. The Plaintiff therefore prays for a judgment and decree of this Hon'ble Court:

(a) Directing specific performance of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 by the Defendant in favour of the Plaintiff regarding the Schedule Property;
(b) Directing the Defendant to execute and register the Sale Deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon payment of the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/-;
(c) Awarding compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance;
(d) Granting possession of the Schedule Property to the Plaintiff under Section 22 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963;
(e) Awarding costs of the suit; and
(f) Granting such other and further reliefs as this Hon'ble Court deems fit in the interests of justice.

SCHEDULE OF PROPERTY

All that piece and parcel of the residential property bearing Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, more particularly described in the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

1. Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 (Original)

2. RTGS Receipt dated 10.01.2024 (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

3. Legal Notice dated 20.06.2024 with Postal Receipt

4. Defendant's Refusal Letter dated 05.06.2024

VERIFICATION

I, Smt. Anita Sharma, the Plaintiff herein, do hereby declare that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 20 of the plaint are true and correct to my knowledge and belief. The last paragraph is the prayer to this Hon'ble Court.

Verified at Bengaluru on this {{DATE}} day of {{MONTH}}, 2024.

PLAINTIFF

ADVOCATE FOR PLAINTIFF
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
{{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT_NUMBER}}
{{CHAMBERS_ADDRESS}}
{{CONTACT_DETAILS}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION) AT BENGALURU, KARNATAKA

Commercial Suit No. ___ of 2024

BETWEEN:

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act,
Having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon, aged 40 years.

... PLAINTIFF

AND:

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A limited liability partnership having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh, aged 48 years.

... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND INTEREST

The Plaintiff above named submits as follows:

JURISDICTION

1. This is a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, arising out of a Software Development Agreement entered into between the parties. The specified value of the subject matter is Rs. 32,00,000/-, which is not less than {{COMMERCIAL_COURTS_THRESHOLD}}, hence this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to try this suit.

2. The cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court as the Defendant has its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru, and the agreement was performed and breached within Bengaluru.

3. This Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the Defendant resides and carries on business within the local limits of this Court's jurisdiction.

4. This Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, to try all suits of a civil nature unless expressly barred.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff is a company registered under the Companies Act, having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru, represented by its Director Priya Menon, aged 40 years. The Defendant is a limited liability partnership having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru, represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh, aged 48 years. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for development of an ERP system for a contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-.

6. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- as first milestone payment via NEFT (UTR: ICIC20240201456789) to the Defendant.

7. As per the Agreement, the Defendant was to deliver the first module by 01.05.2024.

8. The Defendant failed to deliver any module by the deadline of 01.05.2024.

9. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project without returning the advance payment.

10. The Plaintiff suffered additional loss of Rs. 12,00,000/- due to delay in operations caused by non-delivery.

11. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant calling upon them to refund the advance and pay damages.

12. The Defendant has failed to comply with the legal notice or refund the amount.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 37 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the parties are bound to perform their respective promises under the Agreement dated 01.02.2024.

14. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 39 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant refused to perform their promise to deliver the module by 01.05.2024.

15. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 55 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant failed to perform the contract within the fixed time stipulated.

16. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, to claim compensation for loss caused by the breach of contract.

17. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 74 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, regarding compensation for breach of contract where penalty is stipulated.

18. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, for the claim of interest on the principal amount.

19. The suit is governed by Article 55 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

20. The period of limitation is three years. The cause of action accrued on 15.06.2024 when the contract was broken. The suit is filed within the period of limitation.

CAUSE OF ACTION

21. The cause of action arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant abandoned the project without delivery or refund.

22. The cause of action is a single accrual cause of action arising on the date the contract was broken.

LIMITATION

23. The suit is within limitation as per Article 55 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

24. The period is three years from the date when the contract is broken. The breach occurred on 15.06.2024.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

25. The suit is valued for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee at Rs. 32,00,000/-.

26. The Plaintiff pays court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} on the plaint.

PRE-INSTITUTION MEDIATION COMPLIANCE

27. The Plaintiff states that it has complied with the mandatory pre-institution mediation requirement under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, by {{MEDIATION_COMPLIANCE_STATUS}}.

PRAYER

28. The Plaintiff prays for judgment and decree against the Defendant for:

(a) Refund of advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/-;
(b) Consequential damages of Rs. 12,00,000/-;
(c) Interest at 18% per annum on the principal amount from 01.02.2024 till realization;
(d) Costs of the suit; and
(e) Any other relief this Hon'ble Court deems fit.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

29. The Plaintiff relies upon the following documents:

(a) Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024;
(b) NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789);
(c) Email correspondence showing abandonment;
(d) Loss assessment report by auditor;
(e) Legal notice dated 01.07.2024.

VERIFICATION

30. I, Priya Menon, Director of TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 30 are true to my knowledge and belief and based on legal advice which I believe to be true.

31. Verified at Bengaluru on this ___ day of ___, 2024.

STATEMENT OF TRUTH (COMMERCIAL COURTS ACT, 2015)

32. The Plaintiff states that this plaint is verified in accordance with Order VI Rule 15A of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, and Appendix-I of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

33. The Plaintiff confirms that no part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

34. Verified at Bengaluru on this ___ day of ___, 2024.

ADVOCATE FOR PLAINTIFF

[Name of Advocate]
[Enrollment Number]
[Chamber Address]
[Contact Details]
Bengaluru
